question.text: Overlap of pz and pz along internuclear axis (z) gives ____ bond.
KMnO4 acts as an ____ agent in acid, neutral and alkaline media.
Bacteriostatic antibiotics ____ the bacteria.
Ratio of Cp for He and H2 (at room temp) is ____.
Which of the following is an example of Foam?
Reactance of a capacitor to steady DC is ____.
Anhydrous CaCl2 or MgCl2 are added to the electrolyte in Al extraction to ____.
question.explanation: They also help in lowering the melting point.

question.text: Question 102 (Hard) on Ecosystem: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 102 regarding Ecosystem.

question.text: Wheatstone bridge is balanced when ____.
Internal energy increase equals work only if process is ____.
Which hormone mimics the effect of long days in many plants?
question.explanation: Can induce flowering even under short-day conditions.

question.text: Does Kreb's cycle happen in bacteria?
question.explanation: Prokaryotes lack mitochondria but have metabolic equivalent enzymes.

question.text: Movement of water into xylem is mostly ____ in humid conditions? No. Root pressure dominated.
Main plant body of Pteridophytes and higher plants is ____.
question.explanation: Differentiated into true roots, stem and leaves.

question.text: Product of 1,2-dibromoethane reacting with Zn dust? ____.
Most probable speed v_mp = sqrt( ____ ).
Question 41 (Medium) on Biotechnology: Principles and Processes: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 41 regarding Biotechnology: Principles and Processes.

question.text: Question 45 (Medium) on Evolution: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 45 regarding Evolution.

question.text: Degree of dissociation (alpha) = (Moles dissociated) / (____).
Hormone often called 'stress hormone'? ____.
question.explanation: Inhibits plant metabolism and promotes dormancy.

question.text: A dipole has ____ net charge.
Which of the following does not occur in Interphase?
question.explanation: Occurs in M phase; in interphase, DNA is in form of chromatin.

question.text: Compressibility measures volume ____.
Dimension of Area is:
Two vectors A=2i+J and B=i+2j. Dot product? ____.
Bidder's organ in male frog is found in ____.
Atoms of the same element with different mass numbers are ____.
Ratio of radius of first and second Bohr orbits is ____.
Additional practice question 120 on Mineral Nutrition: Advanced level concepts.
question.explanation: Detailed explanation for additional question 120 in the Mineral Nutrition module.

question.text: Question 58 (Medium) on Human Health and Disease: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 58 regarding Human Health and Disease.

question.text: Entropy of a gas is generally ____ than the same substance as liquid.
Metabolic activity is a defining property? ____.
Question 104 (Hard) on Environmental Issues: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 104 regarding Environmental Issues.

question.text: H2 + Br2 <=> 2HBr. Reaction is exothermic. To increase yield ____ temperature.
Incompressible fluid limit for Poisson's ratio is ____.
Field lines of a single negative charge can be thought of ending at infinity? No. They start at infinity.
Why is reproduction not a defining property of life?
Symbiotic association of fungi with roots of higher plants is ____.
Body of Cockroach is divided into three regions: Head, ____ and ____.
question.explanation: Thorax consists of Prothorax, Mesothorax and Metathorax.

question.text: Majority of bacteria are ____ (as per nutrition).
question.explanation: Vital for breaking down organic matter.

question.text: Gravity of Sun at Earth's orbit is ____ than Earth's surface gravity.
PAN stands for ____ in the context of photochemical smog.
Question 40 (Easy) on Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 40 regarding Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants.

question.text: Relative density of Diamond vs Graphite? ____ is more dense.
Question 45 (Medium) on Biotechnology and its Applications: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 45 regarding Biotechnology and its Applications.

question.text: Hollow cylinder torque formula includes ____ factor.
Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter (Chapter 109) complete.
Question 5 (Easy) on Biodiversity and Conservation: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 5 regarding Biodiversity and Conservation.

question.text: Gyroscope precision rate Omega is proportional to ____.
Drift velocity (v_d) is the average velocity of ____ in a conductor.
question.explanation: Acquired due to applied electric field.

question.text: The complex [Pt(en)2Cl2] can show ____ isomerism.
Is it possible for a carbohydrate to be a heteropolymer?
question.option: Yes (Complex ones with nitrogen/sulfur like Chitin, Peptidoglycan)

question.text: Central metal atom in chlorophyll is ____.
Chemotaxonomy uses ____ of organisms.
Filarial worm name? ____.
Neurospora (Ascomycete) is used extensively in ____.
Radius ratio (r+/r-) for tetrahedral void is ____.
Gold leaf electroscope is used for ____ charge.
Question 21 (Easy) on Ecosystem: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 21 regarding Ecosystem.

question.text: Question 53 (Medium) on Human Health and Disease: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 53 regarding Human Health and Disease.

question.text: Question 30 (Easy) on Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 30 regarding Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants.

question.text: Myofibrils in striated muscle show alternate light ____ and dark ____ bands.
question.explanation: Based on Actin and Myosin protein distribution.

question.text: The slope of Ellingham diagram lines for most metal oxides is ____.
question.explanation: Because entropy decreases when gas is consumed.

question.text: Purification of colloids by separating impurities using a membrane is ____.
question.explanation: Based on difference in diffusion rates.

question.text: Sarcolemma is the plasma membrane of ____.
Whiptail disease in cauliflower is due to ____ deficiency.
Additional practice question 116 on Biomolecules: Advanced level concepts.
question.explanation: Detailed explanation for additional question 116 in the Biomolecules module.

question.text: Long Day Plants (LDPs) flower when day length is ____ than critical duration.
question.explanation: Require more light (e.g., radish, sugar beet).

question.text: Stable oxidation state of Lead is ____ due to inert pair effect.
Correct order of IE1: Li ____ Be ____ B ____ C.
Total energy of n identical capacitors in series is ____ times single energy (V same for total).
Question 80 (Medium) on Human Health and Disease: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 80 regarding Human Health and Disease.

question.text: Question 119 (Hard) on Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 119 regarding Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production.

question.text: Bakelite used for electrical switches because it is a ____.
question.option: Poor conductor of heat and electricity

question.text: Flow state if Re changes 500 to 1500 is typically ____.
Question 44 (Medium) on Human Reproduction: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 44 regarding Human Reproduction.

question.text: Question 21 (Easy) on Biotechnology and its Applications: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 21 regarding Biotechnology and its Applications.

question.text: Atomic radius ____ along a period from left to right.
question.explanation: Due to increase in effective nuclear charge.

question.text: Boiling points of carbonyl compounds are ____ than hydrocarbons of same mass.
Glands of the stomach are called ____ glands.
Question 50 (Medium) on Human Health and Disease: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 50 regarding Human Health and Disease.

question.text: Paraldehyde is a trimer of ____.
Gibberellins are synthesized from ____ units (terpenoid pathway).
For an ideal solution, the volume change on mixing (ΔVmix) is ____.
question.explanation: Particles occupy the same space as in pure components.

question.text: Question 70 (Medium) on Principles of Inheritance and Variation: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 70 regarding Principles of Inheritance and Variation.

question.text: Gold number of a protective colloid is ____ proportional to its protective power.
question.explanation: Lower gold number means higher efficiency.

question.text: Can we store AgNO3 solution in a Copper vessel?
question.explanation: Cu is more reactive than Ag.

question.text: If a magnet is broken into pieces, each piece is a ____ magnet.
question.explanation: Atomic dipoles realign to form new poles.

question.text: Escape v from Moon surface approx? ____ km/s.
Activation energy of a reaction is zero. The value of k will ____ with temp.
question.explanation: k = A e^0 = A (independent of T).

question.text: For radioactive decay λ, the probability that a nucleus decays in internal dt is ____.
question.explanation: First order approximation for small dt.

question.text: Bond between two monosaccharides in a polysaccharide is ____ bond.
question.explanation: Formed by dehydration between hydroxyl groups.

question.text: Total number of questions in this standard file? ____.
Chyme is food mixed with ____.
Q114: Glaucoma is due to increased intraocular pressure in ____ humor.
Movement of ions across membrane proteins against gradient is ____.
Neurons with one axon and one dendrite are ____.
Solubility of sulfates of Group 2 ____ down the group.
question.explanation: BeSO4 and MgSO4 are highly soluble.

question.text: Is it possible for a series LCR circuit to have Z < R?
Inner ear is a fluid-filled coiled structure called ____.
question.explanation: Consists of bony and membranous parts.

question.text: The majority of HCO3- is transported from RBC to plasma via ____ exchanger.
Plants that flower once in a life time and then die are ____.
Ploidy levels: Gamete is ____, Zygote is ____.
Intensity of field I at center of 3 particle triangle side L mass M? ____.
Question 79 (Medium) on Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 79 regarding Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production.

question.text: Human digestive system consists of ____ and ____.
Pitcher of Nepenthes is modified ____.
question.explanation: Leaf blade develops into a trap.

question.text: Alkaloid examples include ____ and ____.
Can we determine the exact boiling point of an azeotrope from pure component BPs?
question.option: No (It is lower/higher than either pure component)

question.text: Which noble gas has the least tendency to form compounds?
Value of electrostatic constant k (in vacuum) is ____.
Reimer-Tiemann reaction: Phenol + CHCl3 + aq. NaOH followed by H+ gives ____.
If ΔH is negative and ΔS is positive, reaction is spontaneous at ____.
Question 111 (Hard) on Microbes in Human Welfare: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 111 regarding Microbes in Human Welfare.

question.text: In phloem, sugar is transported as ____.
Allosteric inhibitors bind to a site ____ the active site.
question.explanation: Change the shape of active site.

question.text: Photorespiration occurs across three organelles: Chloroplast, ____ and Mitochondria.
Question 68 (Medium) on Biotechnology and its Applications: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 68 regarding Biotechnology and its Applications.

question.text: Value of i for K3[Fe(CN)6] is ____.
In cationic detergents, the hydrophilic head is ____ charged.
Can a magnetic field change the speed of a particle?
Final check for Morphology: 120 count reached?
A heavier particle from two particles with same velocity has ____ wavelength.
If a soft iron core is inserted into a solenoid, its self-inductance ____.
question.explanation: μ_r >> 1 for iron enhances flux linkage.

question.text: Molecularity is the number of reacting species colliding ____ to bring about reaction.
question.explanation: A theoretical concept for elementary reactions.

question.text: Group of related species is called ____.
question.explanation: Example: Panthera leo, P. pardus, P. tigris.

question.text: Urotelic animals show ____ heart? No. ____ metabolism?
Bohr's quantization condition was explained by De-Broglie by assuming electrons to be ____ waves.
question.explanation: Condition n λ = 2πr ensures constructive self-interference.

question.text: Number of mitotic divisions required to produce 100 cells? ____.
question.explanation: N-1 rule for division events (not generations).

question.text: Artificial sweetener only stable at low temperature is ____.
Process of oxidation of metals in presence of air and moisture is ____.
Question 63 (Medium) on Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 63 regarding Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants.

question.text: The error in measurement of radius of sphere is 1%. Error in volume is:
Additional practice question 114 on Morphology of Flowering Plants: Advanced level concepts.
question.explanation: Detailed explanation for additional question 114 in the Morphology of Flowering Plants module.

question.text: Reaction [ArN2]+ X- + H2O (warm) -> ____.
In lichens, phycobiont is ____ and mycobiont is ____.
question.explanation: Alga provides food, Fungus provides shelter and minerals.

question.text: Small bristle-like fibers in bacteria for attachment to rocks/host? ____.
Potential energy of diatomic molecule U(r) near minimum approximates ____.
Mole fraction of solvent in a solution where x_solute = 0.2 is ____.
Enzymes that catalyze transfer of groups (e.g. C, N, P) are ____.
Snapping wire temperature rise origin is ____.
Question 95 (Hard) on Reproductive Health: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 95 regarding Reproductive Health.

question.text: Question 29 (Easy) on Biotechnology and its Applications: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 29 regarding Biotechnology and its Applications.

question.text: Pigmented and opaque structure visible through cornea? ____.
question.explanation: Contains muscles that regulate pupil size.

question.text: Question 15 (Easy) on Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 15 regarding Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production.

question.text: Question 24 (Easy) on Biodiversity and Conservation: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 24 regarding Biodiversity and Conservation.

question.text: If light from a laser passes through two slits, is it coherent?
question.explanation: Lasers provide high temporal and spatial coherence.

question.text: Which of the following reacts with ammoniacal Silver Nitrate (Tollen's reagent)?
question.explanation: Forms white precipitate of silver acetylide.

question.text: Main function of transpiration is ____.
A person whistle while moving at v/2 towards wall. Echo freq to whistle freq ratio is ____.
question.explanation: Double Doppler (source to wall, wall to moving obs).

question.text: Which of the following has the shortest wavelength in the EM spectrum?
Auxetic material Poisson's Ratio is ____.
Relationship between linear and volume expansion for isotropic solid: γ = ____.
Organelle known as 'Kitchen of the cell' in plants is ____.
System of adjacent cell walls and intercellular spaces is ____.
Scales of cartilaginous fishes are ____ scales.
Question 79 (Medium) on Biotechnology and its Applications: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 79 regarding Biotechnology and its Applications.

question.text: A flower with four distinct whorls (Calyx, Corolla, Androecium, Gynoecium) is ____.
Is glucose reabsorbed in the loop of Henle?
question.option: No (reabsorption complete by end of PCT)
question.explanation: In a healthy individual, all glucose is back in blood after PCT.

question.text: Organisms belonging to Kingdom Monera are ____.
question.explanation: Lack a well-defined nucleus and membrane-bound organelles.

question.text: Question 92 (Hard) on Human Health and Disease: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 92 regarding Human Health and Disease.

question.text: Relationship: v_mp < v_avg < ____.
Question 40 (Easy) on Evolution: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 40 regarding Evolution.

question.text: Question 42 (Medium) on Biodiversity and Conservation: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 42 regarding Biodiversity and Conservation.

question.text: Transition metals and their compounds act as good ____.
question.explanation: Due to variable oxidation states and complex formation ability.

question.text: Question 27 (Easy) on Biodiversity and Conservation: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 27 regarding Biodiversity and Conservation.

question.text: Question 4 (Easy) on Human Reproduction: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 4 regarding Human Reproduction.

question.text: Catecholamines increase heart beat, strength of heart contraction and rate of ____.
Structure of EDTA4- ion contains how many donor sites?
In the above series cells, moles of Cu deposited?
Uranium-235 undergoes fission by ____ neutrons.
question.explanation: Thermal neutrons have higher probability of capture.

question.text: Specific heat Cv of hydrogen at high temp is higher due to ____ modes.
Question 32 (Easy) on Molecular Basis of Inheritance: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 32 regarding Molecular Basis of Inheritance.

question.text: Force between plates of parallel plate capacitor with dielectric K is F' = ____.
question.explanation: Field E is reduced to E/K, or U changes.

question.text: Question 77 (Medium) on Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 77 regarding Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants.

question.text: Question 81 (Hard) on Molecular Basis of Inheritance: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 81 regarding Molecular Basis of Inheritance.

question.text: Van't Hoff factor (i) for association is ____.
Additional practice question 101 on Cell Cycle and Cell Division: Advanced level concepts.
question.explanation: Detailed explanation for additional question 101 in the Cell Cycle and Cell Division module.

question.text: Which of the following nitrogen oxides is isoelectronic with CO2?
Property of a body by virtue of which it returns to its original configuration after removal of deforming force is ____.
question.explanation: Fundamental property of elastic materials like steel.

question.text: Percentage of sulphur in vulcanized tire rubber is approx ____.
Ψw of a plasmolyzed cell is ____ than its environment? No.
Twisting couple per unit twist formula is ____.
The two systems that jointly coordinate and integrate all activities of organs are Neural and ____.
Yeast poison themselves to death when alcohol concentration reaches ____%.
Yellow color of NaCl when heated in Na vapor is due to ____ units.
question.explanation: Absorption of light in visible range.

question.text: Inner parts of cerebral hemispheres and associated structures like Amygdala/Hippocampus? ____.
question.explanation: Involved in emotional behavior and memory.

question.text: Equivalent weight of an acid = Molecular weight / ____.
In photoelectric effect, emission occurs within ____ seconds of illumination.
question.explanation: Suggests direct collision of photons and electrons.

question.text: The ratio of peak voltage V₀ to peak current I₀ in LCR circuit is ____.
Number of carbons in 3-PGA is ____.
Magnetic field lines of a solenoid resemble those of a ____ magnet.
question.explanation: Internal field is uniform; external field is weak.

question.text: Question 115 (Hard) on Ecosystem: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 115 regarding Ecosystem.

question.text: The quantity (wt + phi) is called the ____ of the SHM.
question.explanation: Determines state of motion at time t.

question.text: Question 102 (Hard) on Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 102 regarding Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production.

question.text: Two tuning forks of frequencies f1 and f2 produce 5 beats/s. If f2 is loaded with wax, beat freq decreases. This means ____.
question.explanation: Waxing lowers freq. Gap (f1 - f2) shrinks if f1 > f2.

question.text: The speed of electromagnetic waves in vacuum is c = ____.
Question 49 (Medium) on Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 49 regarding Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants.

question.text: If an element with atomic mass 100 has BCC structure and edge 400pm, density is approx ____ g/cm3.
Pteridophyte that produces two kinds of spores (Heterosporous) is ____.
